sourceRootLabel: AgenC WP-05 checklist and daemon background agent JSON-RPC contract

requiredBehaviors.behavior: The shared portal protocol allowlist exposes agent.create, agent.list, and agent.stop for the web background agent dashboard while continuing to reject daemon methods outside the portal surface.
edgeCases: portal method guards accept agent.create and agent.stop after WP-05
portal method guards still reject tool mutation methods outside the dashboard scope
initialize advertises explicit list, start, and stop capability flags alongside the existing workspace flags

requiredBehaviors.behavior: Portal request builders serialize agent.list, agent.create, and agent.stop into daemon JSON-RPC envelopes with stable ids and params suitable for browser dashboard actions.
edgeCases: agent.list includes optional pagination params without changing the method name
agent.create preserves initial content, unattended policy, and metadata for dashboard-started agents
agent.create omits undefined optional metadata instead of sending ambiguous JSON fields
agent.stop includes an operator-visible reason when the dashboard stops an agent

requiredBehaviors.behavior: Portal dashboard snapshots model the background-agent list and in-flight start/stop control state separately from transcript and composer state.
edgeCases: dashboard snapshots carry the daemon AgentStatus values used by agent.list results
an empty stoppingAgentIds list represents no in-flight stop without changing layout state
dashboard snapshots preserve top-level workspace agents for WP-04 compatibility while adding the WP-05 backgroundAgents state

requiredBehaviors.behavior: The daemon dispatcher accepts initialized portal JSON-RPC requests for background-agent list, start, and stop and routes them through the existing background agent manager and runner.
edgeCases: the initialized portal connection records the same capability flags the browser sends
agent.list returns an empty dashboard list before any background agents are started
agent.create trims the objective and seeds a daemon session for the new background agent
agent.stop forwards the dashboard stop reason to the runner and removes the agent from subsequent list results

requiredBehaviors.behavior: The sibling web portal renders background-agent list, start, and stop controls backed by daemon agent.list, agent.create, and agent.stop.
edgeCases: the portal initializes with protocol 0.4 capability flags for list, start, and stop
hostile daemon strings in background-agent rows render through textContent
start submits normalized objective, cwd, and dashboard metadata through agent.create
start and stop controls disable while lifecycle actions are in flight
empty objectives and empty stop reasons are rejected before daemon requests are sent
stop forwards an operator-visible reason and refreshes the list after the daemon removes the agent

intentionalReductions: WP-05 does not add a hosted portal backend; browser actions continue to target the initialized daemon WebSocket from WP-02.
WP-05 does not add permission approval, tool cancellation, or live transcript streaming controls to the background agent dashboard.
The sibling portal repository is recorded as evidence, but this item's executable contract lives in agenc-core because the daemon protocol package owns the stable request surface.

mergeResolutions.context: WP-05 exposes daemon background-agent lifecycle methods as stable portal dashboard actions.
mergeResolutions.resolution: Added portal method/capability entries, request builders, dashboard state types, and dispatcher coverage that uses the portal builders against the daemon JSON-RPC connection.
